name: Bill Saxton
abstract: William Edward Saxton  (born June 28, 1946, in New York City) is an American hard bop tenor saxophonist. He studied clarinet, composition and arrangement at the New England Conservatory in Boston, graduating in 1973, and worked with Pharoah Sanders, Jackie McLean and Bennie Maupin.

concerts.label: JVC Jazz Festival: Patti LaBelle
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Foster
about_quotes.evidence: Bill Saxton, who’s a stalwart and veteran of one of the earlier Loud Minority bands. ... Exactly, yeah. ... Bill Saxton ... That’s what the Swing Plus dance band consists of.
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Foster — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
